content: The grant proposal application requires a title page with the research title, PI information (name, institute, research authority), application date, co-investigator information (name, institute), and signatures of the PI and research authority. Indicate if the project is associated with a commercial company or external parties.

Include abstracts in English, Hebrew, and Layman's terms (either Hebrew or English), each up to one page, covering background and aim, methods, expected results, importance to medicine, and five keywords.

The research plan should be up to 10 pages for proposals (3 pages for pre-proposals), excluding references, and should include scientific background, research question, study design (detailed plan, methods, sample size justification, available equipment), preliminary results, schematic representation of proposal (partner responsibilities, time scheme), and a time estimate for each stage. References should be up to 15.
The budget should be described for the WHOLE research duration, in accordance to the budget items in the electronic form. It must include Person Power costs. Overhead is 10% of the final sum.
Salaries to PI and CoI, permanent equipment (including computers and software), clinics charges, and travels abroad are not eligible costs.
Justify the budget, especially if the project receives support from another grant or a similar application was made.
Provide a Curriculum Vitae form for each applicant, including education, employment, and active research grants/contracts.
Include collaboration letters from collaborators consenting to contribute to the research.
Provide names and email addresses of at least 5 experts in the field, avoiding those with conflicts of interest.

The proposal should be compiled in the specified order, including a printed copy of the complete electronic form, abstracts (Hebrew, English, and Layman's terms), research plan, budget, CVs of all researchers, collaboration letters, bio-ethics approvals (if applicable), and a list of potential reviewers.
cfp_subject: This call for proposals outlines the requirements for grant applications related to medical research, detailing the necessary components such as title page information, structured abstracts, comprehensive research plans, itemized budgets, researcher CVs, collaboration confirmations, ethical compliance documentation, and suggested expert reviewers. It targets researchers in the medical field seeking funding for their projects.
